MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Oct-17-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
David B. Golden, Esq. Golden Kopcke, T:415.399.9994, for defendant/moving party;
Johnny D. Knadler, Esq., T:310.564.6695, for plaintiff/opposing party.

RULING: The tentative ruling was contested and argued. Plaintiff's counsel requested that payment of the sanctions be made a condition for granting the motion. Defendant's counsel agreed to pay the sanctions immediately after the hearing. After arguments, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on DEFENDANTS AYMAN NESHEIWAT and MARILYN ZEIDAN's MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND, as follows:

Defendants Nesheiwat and Zeidan's motion to vacate the default entered on August 23, 2019 is granted. Defendants have moved for relief within a reasonable time, lodged a proposed answer, and demonstrate mistake and/or excusable neglect. (See Code Civ. Proc., 473(b).) There is a liberal policy to hear cases on the merits. Defendants were acting in pro per when they were served with the second amended complaint. The second amended complaint did not include a summons warning defendants that they needed to answer again. It was not unreasonable for defendants, who are not versed in the law, to believe that their answer to the first amended complaint would constitute a sufficient response to the second amended complaint. Defendants shall remit $1,000 in fees to plaintiff by December 1, 2019 as reasonable fees for obtaining the default. (See Code Civ. Proc., 473(c)(1)(C); Rogalski v. Nabers Cadillac (1992) 11 Cal.App.4th 816, 823.) Defendants shall file their answer by October 25, 2019.

Order signed in open court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com
